event.summary: Accepted the unit after passing validation and instruction synchronization.

acceptance.proof: One public input family covers exactly the seven existing TimestampedChunk<T> representations. Accepted non-empty state retains the original ChunkLimits and an ordered vector of unforgeable CORE-006 bindings, with every input sample delegated exactly once through BoundTimestampedDescriptorSample::new.

Accepted state preserves sample count, homogeneous values, channel order, raw timestamp bits, optional derived absence or presence, derived kind and bits, per-sample pairing, chunk order, and original chunk limits exactly without cloning values, reading clocks, calculating timestamps, sorting, splitting, merging, or rewriting evidence.

Focused tests cover all seven exact chunk mappings, deterministic non-empty rejection, multi-sample order and pairing, format and channel mismatches at sample zero, a later String-bound failure with exact sample and channel indexes, first-failure sample order, and unchanged delegated CORE-006 error payloads and precedence.

The crate remains one unpublished std-only package with no dependencies, features, unsafe code, clocks, timestamp algorithms, conversion, encoding, XML, buffering, queueing, runtime effects, protocol implementation, or compatibility overclaim.

document.commit_policy: Commit the accepted timestamped descriptor/chunk composition separately from empty-chunk compatibility, clocks, timestamp algorithms, splitting, merging, buffering, queues, XML, discovery, protocol, transport, adapters, providers, or runtime work.

instruction_surfaces.change_reason: Route timestamped descriptor/chunk binding while keeping chunk ownership data-only and buffering, clock, wire, transport, and runtime authority closed.
Describe only ordered local chunk composition, exact evidence preservation, and indexed delegated errors without claiming queues, buffering, transport, protocol, or ecosystem support.

Review whether ordered composition and indexed delegated failure add reusable evidence guidance; avoid adding Rusty-LSL-specific chunk recipes when existing contract-composition guidance is sufficient.
instruction_surfaces.validation: Review the installed skill and run its instruction-surface whitespace check.

non_scope: Empty-chunk compatibility behavior, implicit descriptor validation without a sample, chunk splitting or merging, rechunking, buffering, queues, capacity planning, backpressure, timeouts, scheduling, retries, recovery, or runtime lifecycle.
Clock reads, clock correction, dejittering, smoothing, interpolation, timestamp derivation, nominal-rate enforcement, timestamp sorting, timestamp rewriting, or postprocessing algorithms.

Numeric or string conversion, byte sizing, encoding, decoding, endianness, memory layout, XML, discovery, networking, sockets, inlets, outlets, transport, threads, providers, FFI, native loading, or language bindings.
Cargo dependencies, Cargo features, unsafe Rust, additional crates, generated protocol material, copied or translated liblsl or rLSL source, and production liblsl integration.

Morphospace adapters, Manifold admission or stream authority, platform lifecycle, Quest or Hostess behavior, device validation, and any algorithmic, protocol, wire, runtime, performance, layout, or ecosystem compatibility claim.
document.objective: Implement a dependency-free accepted contract that binds one existing non-empty timestamped homogeneous chunk to a validated stream descriptor by delegating every ordered sample through CORE-006, preserving chunk limits, values, timestamps, order, pairings, and indexed delegated errors without adding buffering, clocks, algorithms, transport, or runtime behavior.
outputs: Dependency-free timestamped descriptor/chunk input, accepted-state, and typed deterministic indexed delegated-error APIs for exactly seven homogeneous chunk families.
Positive and damaged tests plus a separate CORE-007 local-results overlay preserving every historical STRM-000 result.

Deterministic CORE-007 validation entrypoint and public status updates that do not claim empty-chunk compatibility, clocks, timestamp algorithms, conversion, buffering, queues, protocol, transport, runtime, wire, or ecosystem support.
